title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 04, July 1884, No. 10
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: They left by the last train at 12.40 p.m., the main body having left at
quoteLast: This was our farewell to a district of which we had got to know practically every inch, and of which we shall always retain most happy recollections.
title:
